# Modifications I Made to the Source Code

## View Synthetic Images Generated
To view the synthetic images generated I added 2 lines to dast.py. 
```bash
    # Creates directory to store images generated by the model
    os.makedirs('images', exist_ok=True)
```
This makes a directory to store the images. The next line I added Saves the synthetic images the generator produces after a set amnt of epochs are done within a 10x10 grid. Since I am testing this using the MNIST dataset thats one image per digit class so 10 samples x 10 classes = 100 images. 
```bash
    # Prints 10x10 grid of the synthetic images generated
    if epoch % 10 == 0:
        vutils.save_image(data[:100].detach(), 'images/epoch_%04d.png' % epoch, normalize=True, nrow=10)
```

## Live Log Output
The code was originally setup to print the log at the end of training. If the training gets interrupted whatever output that was sitting in Python's memory buffer gets lost. Within dast.py I edited the logger object, more specifically the flush function so you can now see log output in real time. This also prevents anything from getting lost if the job gets preempted as I was using an HPC.
```bash
    def flush(self):
        self.terminal.flush()
        self.log.flush()
```

## Comments on Files I used During my Expirementation with the Codebase
Added comments to the files I used so I could reference back to them. The files I used in order to run the code were dast.py, net.py and test_dast.py
